title: pavonia oxyphyllaria
intro: Pavonia oxyphyllaria, also known as rock rose or rose mallow, is a species of flowering plant in the mallow family Malvaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pavonia oxyphyllaria include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in folk medicine to treat various other ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
